_meta.title: Wi-Fi Tools Comparison
_meta.pricingNote: Pricing as of July 2026. Confirm current pricing with the vendor before you buy.
_meta.estimateNote: Cost figures are modeled estimates assembled by WLAN Pros from vendor-supplied numbers and list pricing, not vendor-published quotes. Treat every dollar amount as a planning estimate, not a binding price.
_meta.betaNote: This comparison is in beta review. Vendors are being consulted on the figures, and a few may change before the final release.
_meta.neutralityNote: This is a capability and cost reference, not a ranking. Tools are listed alphabetically within each activity. The same vendor can appear under more than one activity. Real toolkits are assembled across vendors. This reflects vendors interviewed by WLAN Pros, not every tool that exists.
_meta.noLogosNote: No vendor logos or product photos appear here. Those are trademarks and copyrighted images that need each vendor's written permission, which is still being gathered.
_meta.source: Wi-Fi Design, Validation, Spectrum Analysis & Troubleshooting Tools V6 (Keith Parsons, vendor-interviewed workbook, last revised 2026-02-16).

activities.title: Wi-Fi Design
activities.intro: Design is the planning phase, done before climbing a ladder. A designer models a building, places access points on a floor plan, and predicts how Wi-Fi will behave: where coverage reaches, where signal overlaps, which channels go where, and how many APs an area needs. The output is a plan: AP locations, mounting notes, channel and power settings, and a bill of materials. Design answers what to install, and where, before any hardware is bought.

configs.notes: AI Pro desktop and online, with 4G and 5G planning. First year is $5,995 plus $1,995 Connect.
Browser-based design. SaaS model pricing.
Adds live network visibility and private cellular planning. SaaS model pricing.
Perpetual license. Years 2 and 3 support optional.

Sidos Cloud for 1 named user. Network design and planning, survey project management, and AI-powered modeling. Browser-based, no hardware. The Sidos Wave device is only needed to collect on-site data and is priced separately under Validation.

activities.title: Wi-Fi Validation
activities.intro: Validation is the proof step. After a network is installed, or when a designer wants to confirm a predictive model against reality, validation measures the actual RF environment on site and compares it to the design goals. The engineer walks the space collecting real signal strength, signal-to-noise, data rates, and coverage, then produces a heatmap and a report. Validation answers whether the installed network does what was promised.

configs.notes: 4 triband radios with spectrum analysis, the Survey app, and Sidekick support. First year is $4,995 plus $1,295.
Hamina Clip device $990 one-time (includes 6 months of the Hamina app) plus Onsite $780/yr. 2 triband radios; no spectrum analysis.
4 triband radios across a cross-vendor kit. First year is $1,710 plus $780.
Handheld tester. Perpetual license. Years 2 and 3 support optional.

Sidos Wave ($2,388/yr, one device shared across unlimited users, all Micro-Apps including Deep Survey) plus Cloud for 1 named user ($468/yr). 3 triband radios. This config does not include spectrum analysis. Legacy 3-user bundle ($2,988/yr) still available.

activities.title: Spectrum Analysis
activities.intro: Spectrum analysis looks below Wi-Fi at the raw radio frequencies, including energy that Wi-Fi adapters cannot see or report. A spectrum analyzer shows all RF activity in the 2.4, 5, and 6/7 GHz bands, including non-Wi-Fi interference: microwave ovens, Bluetooth, video bridges, motion sensors, and other sources that degrade Wi-Fi without showing up as a network. It answers what else is using the airspace, and whether something non-Wi-Fi is hurting performance.

configs.notes: 4 radios with spectrum analysis, the Survey and Analyzer apps, and Sidekick support. First year is $4,995 plus $1,995.
Triband spectrum analyzer, assembled across vendors. First year is $780 plus $1,710 plus $999.
Triband spectrum analyzer, assembled across vendors. First year is $780 plus $1,710 plus $1,195.
Pairs the Mac analyzer with a NetAlly spectrum accessory: WiFi Explorer Pro 3 ($129.99) plus one NXT-2000 ($1,195). One-time software with the NXT-2000 hardware.
Perpetual license. Years 2 and 3 optional.
Triband spectrum analyzer with 1 year of MetaCare.

activities.title: Wi-Fi Troubleshooting
activities.intro: Troubleshooting is the diagnostic phase, used when a working network starts misbehaving or a new one fails to meet expectations. It pulls together pieces of the other three activities, a quick survey, a spectrum check, packet capture, and connectivity tests, to isolate a root cause: a bad channel plan, an interference source, a misconfigured AP, a wired-side problem, or a client issue. Troubleshooting tools are portable and fast, built to get an answer on site. They answer why a network is not working, and where the fault is.

configs.notes: 4 radios with spectrum analysis, the Survey and Analyzer apps, and Sidekick support.
Hamina Clip device $990 one-time (includes 6 months of the Hamina app) plus Onsite $780/yr. 2 triband radios; no spectrum analysis.
4 triband radios with spectrum analysis, assembled across vendors. First year is $780 plus $1,710 plus $1,195.
Scanning, analysis, and packet capture. Requires macOS or a WLAN Pi for data collection. This config does not include Wi-Fi design or coverage validation.
Perpetual license. Years 2 and 3 support optional.

A fast on-Mac diagnostic toolkit. The NetViews Pro app is $49.99 one-time; the $900 total is a planning estimate for the app plus a 13-inch MacBook Air, not a single SKU. This config does not include spectrum analysis.

configs.product: Chanalyzer + Wi-Spy Lucid + MetaGeek App + Nomad
configs.notes: Oscium Complete Bundle: spectrum and packet tools with the Nomad probe and a directional antenna, plus 1 year of MetaCare. 3-year TCO adds MetaCare renewals in years 2-3 (~$400/yr across the kit: $150 Chanalyzer + $250 MetaGeek App).
Sidos Wave ($2,388/yr, one device shared across unlimited users) plus Cloud for 1 named user ($468/yr). 3 triband radios. Legacy 3-user bundle ($2,988/yr) still available.
Internal Wi-Fi 7 NIC. Open hardware probe for capture and analysis.
External Wi-Fi 6E NIC.

Devices can be shared between users.

toolkits.notes: Oscium Complete Bundle: spectrum and packet tools with the Nomad probe and a directional antenna, plus 1 year of MetaCare. 3-year TCO adds MetaCare renewals in years 2-3 (~$400/yr across the kit).
One Wave ($2,388/yr) shared across unlimited users; Cloud at $468 per named user. Planner-only Cloud is $1,404 over 3 years. Legacy 3-user bundle is $8,964. No spectrum analysis.

vendors.name: Ekahau
vendors.summary: A long-established Wi-Fi design and survey company, now part of Ookla. Its platform centers on Ekahau AI Pro for design and analysis, paired with Connect services and the Sidekick 2 measurement device, plus mobile Survey, Measure, and Analyzer apps. Together the suite spans predictive design, on-site survey and validation, spectrum analysis with the Sidekick, and troubleshooting. The Ekahau figures here were confirmed directly with the vendor in June 2026.

vendors.name: Hamina
vendors.summary: A Wi-Fi design and survey company built around a browser-based platform. Planner handles network design with real-time heatmaps and planning for Wi-Fi, IoT, and private 4G/5G. Onsite is the mobile survey, troubleshooting, and spectrum tool, and Live adds 3D network visibility. The Clip is a purpose-built survey device. Spectrum capability depends on which config is assembled.

vendors.name: Intuitibits
vendors.summary: A Mac-focused wireless software maker founded by Adrian Granados. WiFi Explorer Pro 3 is a professional Wi-Fi scanner and analyzer for designing, validating, and troubleshooting, with filtering and visualization of channel use and interference. Airtool 2 handles Wi-Fi packet capture with Wireshark integration. A Windows version of WiFi Explorer Pro also exists.

vendors.name: NetAlly
vendors.summary: Makes portable, ruggedized network test instruments for wired and wireless networks, from the former Fluke Networks and NetScout enterprise test line. AirMagnet Survey PRO plans and validates Wi-Fi, AirCheck G3 Pro is a handheld tester, the NXT-2000 is a triband spectrum analyzer accessory used in many cross-vendor kits, and EtherScope nXG is a combined wired and wireless analyzer. NetAlly withdrew its published pricing during a 2026 leadership transition, so the NetAlly figures here are last-known prices, not currently published — expect them to change when NetAlly republishes.

vendors.name: NetViews
vendors.summary: A modern macOS network and Wi-Fi diagnostic app built by network engineer Bill Bushong, formerly named PingStalker. It bundles live network monitoring and scanning, host probing, speed tests, Wi-Fi tools, and calculators into one Mac app. A one-time-purchase app rather than an enterprise survey platform, positioned as a fast on-Mac diagnostic toolkit.

vendors.name: Oscium
vendors.summary: Makes portable RF and spectrum diagnostic hardware and the software that drives it, carrying the MetaGeek lineage of spectrum tools. Wi-Spy Lucid is a triband spectrum analyzer covering roughly 2.2 to 7.25 GHz, Nomad is a wearable multi-radio measurement and capture device, Chanalyzer turns spectrum data into clear graphs and locates interference, and inSSIDer is a long-running Wi-Fi scanner.

vendors.name: Sidos
vendors.summary: A newer entrant offering an integrated Wi-Fi survey and troubleshooting system. The Sidos Wave is a touchscreen multi-band survey instrument supporting 2.4/5/6 GHz, paired with Sidos Cloud for survey data, simulation, and reporting, plus a mobile app and on-device micro-apps that run guided tasks directly on the Wave. The license model splits a shared Wave hardware subscription ($2,388/yr per device, used by unlimited technicians) from per-named-user Cloud access ($468/yr), so Cloud can be bought on its own as a planner.

vendors.name: WLAN Pi
vendors.summary: An open-hardware Wi-Fi probe and toolkit used widely in the community. The WLAN Pi M4+ carries an internal Wi-Fi 7 NIC, the R4 takes an external Wi-Fi 6E NIC, and the Go is a portable model. It is a flexible capture and analysis platform that frequently pairs with other vendors' software in cross-vendor toolkits.
